text.title: event-sourcing-snapshots Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-snapshots as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Persisting a snapshot and then skipping event replay entirely when loading — "if snapshot exists, return snapshot state directly".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This makes the snapshot the source of truth. When event schema changes, the snapshot encodes old field semantics. The system silently returns wrong aggregate state for any aggregate whose last snapshot predates the schema bump.

failure-mode.description: Taking a snapshot on every save instead of every N events.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A snapshot per event doubles the write load and provides no replay benefit — you still load one snapshot and zero events. Snapshot at N=50–100 to amortize the overhead.

failure-mode.description: Deploying a new event class version (renamed/removed fields) without first invalidating existing snapshots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Old snapshots encode state built from old event semantics. Applying new events on top produces wrong state. Always invalidate snapshots as part of the schema-bump migration and rebuild before going live.
